mixStrategy:
This jazz energize arrangement is built around a classic ii-V-I turnaround in F major (Gm7→C7→Fmaj7) followed by Am7→Dm7→Gm7→C7, cycling through 45 seconds with a driving swing feel.

FREQUENCY SEPARATION: The Acoustic Bass (GM 32) anchors the sub-bass and bass region (MIDI 28-45, 20-300Hz) with walking quarter notes that outline roots and chromatic approaches. The Warm Pad (GM 89) fills the harmonic body (MIDI 53-67, 300Hz-2kHz) with sustained 7th/9th chord voicings that change on the downbeat of each chord. The Acoustic Piano melody (GM 0) soars above in the 1-8kHz presence range (MIDI 65-82) with bebop lines that arpeggiate chord tones on strong beats and use chromatic passing tones on weak beats. Drums provide the full-spectrum rhythmic skeleton with ride cymbal swing, brush snare ghost notes, and kick on beat 1.

RHYTHMIC INTERLOCK: Bass and kick drum lock together on beat 1 of each bar. The ride cymbal provides the swing pulse. The piano melody breathes between ride hits, creating rhythmic conversation. The pad sustains underneath, providing harmonic glue. Ghost notes on snare fill the spaces between bass notes.

ENERGY ARC (6 segments of ~7.5s each): Seg1 (0-7.5s) 0.4 energy — sparse, lower velocities, establishing the groove. Seg2 (7.5-15s) 0.6 — bass walks fuller, melody enters more actively. Seg3 (15-22.5s) 0.8 — denser rhythms, higher velocities, drums more active. Seg4 (22.5-30s) 0.9 — near peak, melody plays continuous bebop lines. Seg5 (30-37.5s) 1.0 — full energy climax, all tracks at maximum density and velocity. Seg6 (37.5-45s) 0.7 — controlled descent, sparser melody, softer dynamics.

HARMONIC PLAN (4-bar phrases at 115 BPM, ~2.09s per bar):
Bars 1-2: Gm7 (ii7) | C7 (V7)
Bars 3-4: Fmaj7 (Imaj7) | Fmaj7
Bars 5-6: Am7 (iii7) | Dm7 (vi7)
Bars 7-8: Gm7 (ii7) | C7 (V7)
This 8-bar cycle (~16.7s) repeats ~2.7 times across 45 seconds, with the energy arc shaping dynamics.
scale: Dorian, Mixolydian, Lydian, Bebop Dominant with chromatic passing tones
